2603.25329.title: Beyond Detection: Rethinking Education in the Age of AI-writing
2603.25329.paper_summary: As generative AI tools like ChatGPT enter classrooms, workplaces and everyday thinking, writing is at risk of becoming a formality -- outsourced, automated and stripped of its cognitive value. But writing is not just output; it is how we learn to think. This paper explores what is lost when we let machines write for us, drawing on cognitive psychology, educational theory and real classroom practices. We argue that the process of writing -- messy, slow, often frustrating -- is where a human deep learning happens. The paper also explores the current possibilities of AI-text detection, how educators can adapt through smarter pedagogy rather than bans, and why the ability to recognize machine-generated language may become a critical literacy of the 21st century. In a world where writing can be faked, learning can not.
2603.25329.paper_summary_zh: 隨著生成式 AI 工具如 ChatGPT 進入教室、工作場所和日常思考，寫作面臨變成一種形式主義的風險——外包、自動化，並剝奪其認知價值。<lb/>但寫作不僅僅是輸出；它是我們學習思考的方式。<lb/>本文探討當我們讓機器為我們寫作時，失去了什麼，並引用了認知心理學、教育理論和實際的課堂實踐。<lb/>我們認為，寫作的過程——混亂、緩慢、常常令人沮喪——是人類深度學習發生的地方。<lb/>本文還探討了當前 AI 文本檢測的可能性，教育者如何通過更智能的教學法而非禁令來適應，以及為什麼識別機器生成語言的能力可能成為21世紀的一項關鍵素養。<lb/>在一個寫作可以被偽造的世界裡，學習卻無法。
2603.25329.author: Maria Marina et.al.
2603.25329.authors: Maria Marina, Alexander Panchenko, Vasily Konovalov
